body:
This PR introduces a local package`PrecompileCI` that precompiles common methods in ClimaAtmos. For now, it only precompiles spaces for different spatial configurations, but this can be expanded to precompile more methods. This all happens in `.buildkite/PrecompileCI/src/PrecompileCI.jl`, and the package is then loaded by the driver to load in the precompiled methods.

This PR also adds a CI driver (`.buildkite/ci_driver.jl`) with the same functionality as the current driver.
The current driver `examples/hybrid/driver.jl` now just runs and plots. It also does not load `PrecompileCI`, so it can still be used in existing workflows.

To use, just import it before ClimaAtmos: 
`julia --project=.buildkite`, then
```julia
using PrecompileCI  # Loads the precompiled methods or precompiles if loaded for the first time
import ClimaAtmos as CA
config = CA.AtmosConfig()
sim = CA.get_simulation(config)
CA.solve_atmos!(sim)
```

I included this as a separate package because precompiling these methods when ClimaAtmos is loaded will add to the TTFX.

To see which methods are actually getting precompiled, see this [gist](https://gist.github.com/nefrathenrici/0d5d7b9799ec6052d02ab201cb444b69) or run:
`julia --project=.buildkite/PrecompileCI`
```julia
using PrecompileTools
PrecompileTools.verbose[] = true
include(".buildkite/PrecompileCI/src/PrecompileCI.jl")
```
title: Add simple precompilation example, split out CI driver

body:
This PR lowers the slurm resource requirements for the calibration test, we could enable the reservation for it now.

The job itself now takes ~20min instead of ~15.
title: Lower slurm requirements for calibration tests
Advection of water with working fluid changes

body: This PR adds `ITime` to ClimaAtmos simulations. This is needed to do accurate long runs.
title: Add ITime
